compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect FILE_IMPACT event type and Jira bridge surfacing
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect the FILE_IMPACT event type, the set/get-file-impact CLI subcommands, the Jira bridge surfacing (issue property + formatted comment), and the consumer refactors.

**Why**: Ensure future agents and contributors can discover FILE_IMPACT semantics through authoritative docs rather than source-code excavation.

**Scope**:
- IN: Update CLAUDE.md (architecture section — add FILE_IMPACT to event type list; Quick Reference if applicable), plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md (entries for set-file-impact, get-file-impact), plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md (if event types are enumerated), ADR for the AcliClient._direct_rest_put() pattern break decision
- OUT: Creating new documentation files beyond the single ADR; runtime code changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md, ticket-cli-reference.md, and ticket-system-v3-architecture.md reflect the FILE_IMPACT event type and the new CLI subcommands
- When this story is complete, an ADR exists in docs/adr/ describing the AcliClient._direct_rest_put() pattern break (why ACLI had no properties subcommand; justification for the direct REST path)
- When this story is complete, the Jira bridge surfacing (property key dso.file_impact + formatted comment dedup) is documented so operators understand the machine-readable + human-visible split

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting and conventions
- [Docs] ADR must include the spike finding (ACLI has no properties subcommand) as its rationale — this is the sole justification for _direct_rest_put()

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: Expand docs scope to include (1) file-impact-v1 migration stamp location and format, plus a rollback procedure (delete stamp + FILE_IMPACT events tagged with migration-marker), documented in ticket-system-v3-architecture.md; (2) ADR for _direct_rest_put() pattern break with ACLI spike reference; (3) BRIDGE_ALERT reason taxonomy — add FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED and FILE_IMPACT_COMMENT_SYNC_FAILED entries (update bridge-alert-event.md contract if present); (4) reducer latest-wins semantics for FILE_IMPACT (chosen in story 2985-f04d). Without these, operators lack a recovery path for bad migrations, bridge dashboards see unknown reasons, and future contributors reverse-engineer the reducer semantics. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude
